messages.content:
Which of the following contributes to the glut of digital messages?

Multiple select question.

Messages are easily copied to many people.


Digital messages are preferred in complex situations.


Digital messages are most effective in ambiguous situations.


Several text messages are often used to transmit the same information found in fewer emails.
Messages are easily copied to many people and several text messages are often used to transmit the same information found in fewer emails.

Fill in the blank question.
Mimicking another person's facial expressions or other nonverbal behaviour such as smiling is known as 
 
.
Mimicking another person's facial expressions or other nonverbal behavior such as smiling is known as mirroring.

Why is the preferred type of digital communication changing?

Multiple select question.

Demands from senior leadership


Demands from clients and customers


Available opportunities


Shifting preferences
Demands from senior leadership, demands from clients and customers, shifting preferences, and available opportunities all contribute to the preferred type of digital communication changing.

The important factors to consider in selecting the most appropriate communication channel in a particular situation include:

Multiple select question.

social acceptance.


media richness.


written communication.


nonverbal communication.
The important factors to consider in selecting the most appropriate communication channel in a particular situation include social acceptance, media richness, and nonverbal communication.

Although email dominates most workplace communication, it may eventually be overtaken by emerging forms of:

Multiple choice question.

social media


emotional contagion


face-to-face communication
social media

__ refers to the extent to which a communication channel requires or allows both sender and receiver to be actively involved.

Multiple choice question.

Synchronicity


Duality


Quality


Emotional contagion
Synchronicity

Why do people engage in flaming?

Multiple select question.

Digital messages can be written and transmitted before the sender’s emotions subside.


Digital media are more impersonal (less social presence) than most other communication channels, which reduces the sender’s empathy and sensitivity when crafting the message.


Digital messages take longer to prepare and deliver, allowing the sender to reflect more calmly on the content before it is sent. 


Senders tend to infer a less respectful interpretation of their digital message than was perceived by the receiver.

Digital messages can be written and transmitted before the sender’s emotions subside,
Digital media are more impersonal (less social presence) than most other communication channels, which reduces the sender’s empathy and sensitivity when crafting the message, and
Senders tend to infer a less respectful interpretation of their digital message than was perceived by the receiver.

Which of the following are included in nonverbal communication?

Multiple select question.

Silence


Facial gestures


Words


Voice intonation
Silence, facial gestures, and voice intonation are included in nonverbal communication.

Silence

Facial gestures

Voice intonation
Silence, facial gestures, and voice intonation are included in nonverbal communication.

__ represents unconscious behaviour. We automatically mimic and synchronize our nonverbal behaviours with other people.

Multiple choice question.

Information overload


Emotional contagion


Social connection


Emotional presence
Emotional contagion

Social acceptance and media richness are:

Multiple choice question.

two sets of factors in choosing communication channels.


social activities in organizations.


part of the feedback in the communication model.


decoding sections of the communication model.
Social acceptance and media richness are two sets of factors in choosing communication channels.
presence refers to how much the communication channel creates psychological closeness to others.
Social presence refers to how much the communication channel creates psychological closeness to others.
Digital messages that communicate hostility and other forms of disrespect is referred to as
Digital messages that communicate hostility and other forms of disrespect are referred to as flaming.

Most information is communicated verbally.

True false question.
True
False
False.
Mimicking another person's facial expressions or other nonverbal behaviour such as smiling is known as
Mimicking another person's facial expressions or other nonverbal behavior such as smiling is known as mirroring.

An important determinant of preference for specific communication channels is

Multiple choice question.

nonverbal communication


emotional contagion


national culture
national culture

What type of communication channel usually have the highest social presence?

Multiple choice question.

A text message


An email


Face-to-face interactions


A policy document uploaded to the company's website
Face-to-face interactions usually have the highest social presence.

Sending a text instead of leaving voicemail to a friend is an example of __ in the selection of a channel of communication.

Multiple choice question.

electronic brainstorming


nonverbal communication


media richness


individual preference
individual preference

__ represents unconscious behaviour. We automatically mimic and synchronize our nonverbal behaviours with other people.

Multiple choice question.

Social connection


Information overload


Emotional contagion


Emotional presence
Emotional contagion

When a situation is routine or clear, holding a special meeting would seem:

Multiple choice question.

like a waste of time


a good idea


an opportunity but not essential


essential
like a waste of time

Social 
 refers to how well the communication medium is approved and supported by the organization, teams, and individuals involved in the exchange.
Social acceptance refers to how well the communication medium is approved and supported by the organization, teams, and individuals involved in the exchange.

Which of the following is a factor that determines senders and receivers' preferences for specific communication channels?

Multiple select question.

Individual's previous success with particular communication channels


Individual's level in the organization


Individual's personality


Individual's values

Individual's previous success with particular communication channels, individual's personality, and individual's values can all be factors that determine senders and receivers' preferences for specific communication channels. However, individual's level in the organization is not necessarily a factor in channel preference.
Channels with high media richness tend to have more social
Channels with high media richness tend to have more social presence.

The highest level of media richness is

Multiple choice question.

a newsletter


face-to-face interaction


financial statements


e-mail
Face-to-face interaction typically offers the highest level of media richness.

Most information is communicated verbally.

True false question.
True
False
True.

According to media richness theory, 
 media are better than 
 media when the communication situation is nonroutine and ambiguous.
According to media richness theory, rich media are better than lean media when the communication situation is nonroutine and ambiguous.
The ability to change another person's attitudes, beliefs, or behaviour using logic, facts, and emotional appeals is called
The ability to change another person's attitudes, beliefs, or behavior using logic, facts, and emotional appeals is called persuasion.

Jenn is replying to a client's text message while listening to a discussion at a large meeting. This refers to:

Multiple choice question.

being sensitive to social cues.


being a workaholic.


be casual and comfortable in social situations.


the ability to multi-communicate.
the ability to multi-communicate.

Research supports the view that persuasion works best in face-to-face interactions because:

Multiple select question.

people are persuaded more often under conditions of high social presence.


people want the interaction to end quickly.


spoken communication is accompanied by nonverbal communication.


the sender receives immediate feedback on whether the message is understood and accepted.

Research supports the view that persuasion works best in face-to-face interactions because people are persuaded more often under conditions of high social presence, spoken communication is accompanied by nonverbal communication, and the sender receives immediate feedback on whether the message is understood and accepted.

__ may reduce the value of media richness associated with a rich channel such as a face-to-face meeting.

Multiple choice question.

Communication proficiency


Inability to multi-communicate


Complex language


Social presence distractions
Social presence distractions may reduce the value of media richness associated with a rich channel such as a face-to-face meeting.

Both the 
 and the 
 have imperfect perceptual processes which create barriers to effective communication.
Both the sender and the receiver have imperfect perceptual processes which create barriers to effective communication.

Media __ refers to the communication channel's data carrying capacity. 

Multiple choice question.

preference


acceptance


norms


richness
Media richness refers to the communication channel's data carrying capacity.

The ambiguity of language is always unintended dysfunctional noise.

True false question.
True
False

False. The ambiguity of language is not always unintended dysfunctional noise. It can occur naturally due to the inherent nature of language and can sometimes be intentional for various communicative purposes.

Specialized words and phrases for specific occupations or groups, known as 
, creates barriers to communication for those who lack the necessary codebook.
Specialized words and phrases for specific occupations or groups, known as jargon, creates barriers to communication for those who lack the necessary codebook.
